    "Milkshake-like emulsion under the oil cap indicating coolant-to-oil contamination",
    "Blown head gasket, cracked cylinder head, or failed oil cooler"

  "actions": [
    "Do not run the engine to prevent bearing damage",

  "rationale": "A 'milkshake' appearance under the oil cap indicates that engine coolant has mixed with the engine oil, creating an emulsion. This is a critical failure typically caused by a blown head gasket, cracked cylinder head, or failed oil cooler, which compromises engine lubrication and can lead to catastrophic engine failure if operated.",
